tweets:
Last thread: I upgraded the Ralph Wiggum Loop with error tracking, learning promotion, and quality gates (ralph-dev).

But ralph-dev is still a linear loop. Retry or give up.

ralph-next replaces that with a graph state machine. Here’s how. 🧵

ralph-dev: PICK → IMPLEMENT → QUALITY → retry or skip.

ralph-next: PICK → IMPLEMENT → QUALITY → REFLECT → DECIDE.

DECIDE can route to: retry, split into subtasks, speculate (parallel strategies), or block. Five possible exits instead of two.

The key addition: REFLECT.

Before deciding what to do, ralph-next classifies the failure across 13 types (lint, typecheck, import, test, build:code, build:config, security, flaky-test, env/tooling, oom, timeout, merge-conflict, runtime).

Each has its own retry budget.

The retry matrix is explicit:

Lint → auto-fix → targeted fix → block (budget: 2)
Test → targeted fix → split subtasks → speculate → block (budget: 3)
Security critical → escalate immediately (budget: 0)

No more “try the same thing 3 times.”

Every attempt gets a trajectory score:

score = (quality_coverage × 0.4) + (first_attempt × 0.2) + (duration_ratio × 0.2) + (learning_value × 0.2)

≥0.7 = PASS. 0.4-0.69 = MARGINAL (ships with warning). <0.4 = FAIL (reroute via DECIDE).

But scoring only matters AFTER the hard quality gate.

If /bs:quality fails, the item cannot PASS regardless of score. No gaming the numbers. Quality gate is binary — score is diagnostic.

Two-stage evaluation prevents score inflation.

The wildcard: SPECULATE.

When retries aren’t working, ralph-next spawns two isolated worktrees in parallel — Strategy A (minimal patch) vs Strategy B (deeper refactor).

First branch that passes quality wins. Loser gets archived. Both fail? Block and move on.

SPLIT breaks stuck items into child tasks.

If a backlog item is too big to land cleanly, DECIDE routes to SPLIT. Source item becomes a “split-parent,” children go back into the queue.

The agent decomposes its own work when scope is the problem.

Everything is logged.

trajectory-log.jsonl: every state transition with timestamps.
evidence/<ITEM_ID>.json: per-item failure history, scores, retry paths.

Post-session, I can see exactly why each item passed, failed, or got blocked. Full audit trail.

Safety rail: 8-transition hard cap per item.

If an item bounces between IMPLEMENT → QUALITY → REFLECT → DECIDE eight times, it’s force-routed to BLOCK. No infinite loops.

Combined with per-class budgets, the worst case is bounded and predictable.

ralph-dev made the loop smart. ralph-next made it deliberate.

The agent doesn’t just retry — it reflects, scores, classifies, and routes. It splits work it can’t land. It races strategies when stuck.

All open, all in Claude Code slash commands.
